_comment: Kodi InfoLabel functions and boolean conditions that take numeric parameters that should NOT be validated as control/window IDs. See https://kodi.wiki/view/List_of_boolean_conditions and https://xbmc.github.io/docs.kodi.tv/master/kodi-base/d5/d11/modules__infolabels_boolean_conditions.html

functions_with_integer_params.description: Position: focused position (integer index), SubItem: subitem offset, CurrentItem: current item offset, SetViewMode: view mode ID, Row: row index, Column: column index
offset: offset from current item (integer), position: absolute position (integer), HasPerformedSeek: seconds elapsed (integer)
Offset/Position: song position in playlist (integer), Exists: position check (integer)
AlarmLessOrEqual: seconds for comparison (integer), IdleTime: elapsed seconds (integer)
Comparison functions that take integer thresholds
ListItem takes an optional offset integer (not a control ID). Note: ListItemAbsolute, ListItemPosition, ListItemNoWrap are handled separately.
Control.HasFocus/IsVisible/IsEnabled/GetLabel take control IDs and SHOULD be validated (removed from exclusion list)

Window query functions that take window IDs. Based on Kodi source: xbmc/GUIInfoManager.cpp lines 7971-7979 mapping to WINDOW_IS_MEDIA, WINDOW_IS, WINDOW_IS_ACTIVE, WINDOW_IS_VISIBLE, WINDOW_IS_DIALOG_TOPMOST, WINDOW_IS_MODAL_DIALOG_TOPMOST, WINDOW_PREVIOUS, WINDOW_NEXT

standalone_functions_with_integers.description: Takes absolute position integer, not control ID
Takes position integer, not control ID
Takes position integer, not control ID
Builtin action to activate a window (takes window ID, but validation is complex - exclude for now)
Builtin action to replace current window (takes window ID, but validation is complex - exclude for now)
Builtin action to close a dialog window (takes window ID, not control ID)
